rulings.Case Title: VISHAL GROVER VS. WILLIAM CAMPBELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder In Cross-Complainant William Campbells Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2016 line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT MARJORIE CAMPBELL's Joinder In Cross-Complainant William Campbells Motion For Leave To Amend Second Amended Cross-Complaint Hearing Required. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VISHAL GROVER VS. WILLIAM CAMPBELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration On Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2016 line 1. DEFENDANT DAVID BELLINGS' Motion For Reconsideration On Motion For Summary Judgment/Adjudication GRANTED as the moving party presented new facts re: Settlement Agreement produced on May 24, 2016. The ownership issue is not "new" and does not meet the standard of CCP 1008. Upon reconsideration the motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is DENIED. There is a triable issue of fact regarding waiver as to the attorney's fees. The settlement agreement on its face does not show that Campbells' claims were not direct, no other evidence or authority is presented by the moving party on this issue. Therefore, moving party failed to shift its burden. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VISHAL GROVER VS. WILLIAM CAMPBELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Bellings Motion For Reconsideration On Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2016 line 1. DEFENDANT NRT WEST, INC. DBA COLDWELL BANKER RESIDENTIAL Joinder In Bellings Motion For Reconsideration On Motion For Summary Judgment GRANTED as the moving party presented new facts re: Settlement Agreement produced on May 24, 2016. The ownership issue is not "new" and does not meet the standard of CCP 1008. Upon reconsideration the Motion for Summary Judgment/Adjudication is DENIED. There is a triable issue of fact regarding waiver as to the attorney's fees. Additionally, the settlement agreement on its face does not show that Campbells' claims were not direct, no other evidence or authority is presented by the moving party on this issue. Therefore, moving party failed to shift its burden.= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, MINC, VS. ECOBUILDING HOLDINGS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Catherine Zhang And Ecobuilding America, Llc'S Motion Of Defendants To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Custodian Of Records, San Francisco Treasurer And Tax Collector
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2016 line 3. Defendants Catherine Zhang And Ecobuilding America, Llc'S Motion Of Defendants To Quash Plaintiffs Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records To Custodian Of Records, San Francisco Treasurer And Tax Collector GRANTED. The subpoena is quashed in its entirety. Documents responsive to the over broad subpoena would include privileged tax records. The fact that defendants have not complied with this Court's June 8, 2016 order is not grounds to allow an over broad subpoena that requests privileged documents. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied for lack of sufficient meet and confer. See Artiga Declaration, at par. 4. Calling opposing counsel one time on a Friday and filing a Motion the following Monday is not a proper meet and confer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN HOUSING CLINIC, MINC, VS. ECOBUILDING HOLDINGS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc'S Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Ecobuilding America, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 28, 2016 line 3. Plaintiff Tenderloin Housing Clinic, Inc'S Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Ecobuilding America, Llc GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Defendants' new objections to the RFPs that were the subject of this Court's June 8, 2016 order are improper and shall be omitted in defendants' amended responses. Any documents withheld or redacted based upon the new improper objections shall be produced or produced without redactions. All objections not initially raised have been waived. Amended, verified responses shall be served, along with responsive documents, within 5 court days of this Order. Sanctions in the amount of $3,100 are awarded against Defendant Ecobuilding America, LLC and its attorney of record, Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy LLP payable within 5 court days of this Order. = (501/REQ)
